title: Cyclophyllum rectinervium
intro: Cyclophyllum rectinervium, also known as the 'Hawaiian rosewood', is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyclophyllum rectinervium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often used to make tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cyclophyllum rectinervium belongs to the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This classification places Cyclophyllum rectinervium within the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Ixoroideae subfamily, to which Cyclophyllum rectinervium is thought to belong. Cyclophyllum rectinervium is a species of flowering plant that is native to Madagascar, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Rubiaceae family. The specific classification of Cyclophyllum rectinervium is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of plant systematics.
